title: Entity relationship: billing schema after the customer split
summary: We split the monolithic `Account` row into `Customer`, `Subscription`, and `Invoice` so that billing state can change without rewriting account profile data.
The new schema also moves invoice line items into a dedicated table so historical invoices stay immutable while subscriptions evolve.

sections.title: Billing entities
text: Each `Customer` now owns one or more `Subscription` rows, and each subscription bills one or more `Invoice` rows over time.
Foreign keys point upstream so a snapshot of an invoice is enough to reconstruct who was billed and which plan it covered.

sections.title: Why the split matters
text: Older code mutated billing state on the `Account` row, which made it hard to keep historical invoices accurate when plans changed.
Now each subscription change creates a new row instead of overwriting state, so invoices keep referencing the subscription that was in effect when they were issued.
Migration tests cover backfilling existing accounts into the new tables and replaying a sample of historical invoices.
